gauge.name: Gasconade River near Rich Fountain

impacts.statement: Near this height, State Highway 89 begins flooding north of State Highway E and just north of the Swan Creek bridge.
Near this height, U.S. Highway 50 near Mount Sterling will be closed in both directions.
Near this height, Missouri Highway 42 east of Vienna will be closed in both directions.
Near this height, Missouri State Route E just west of the juncture with Missouri Highway 89 will be closed in both directions.
Near this height, U.S. Highway 63 will be closed in both directions southeast of Vienna.
Near this height, Missouri State Route BB north of Mount Sterling will be closed in both directions.

County Road 623 northwest of the gage and County Road 727 at Highway D are flooded and closed at this height.  Also, State Highway 42 southwest of Belle and State Highway J east of Fredericksburg are also flooded and closed.  Actual point of inundation may be as low as 22 ft.
Near this height, Missouri State Route K west of Bay will be closed in both directions due to backwater along Second Creek.
House basement along the right bank upstream of the gage begins to flood near this height.
At this height County Road 251 north of Fredericksburg is flooded and closed.
Turner Lane under the State Highway 89 bridge begins flooding near this height.
FLOOD STAGE.  Right bank overflows.  Also, State Highway D begins flooding near the Cooper Hill Conservation Area.
State Route N north of US Highway 50 and north of Freedom begins flooding near this height.
The trailer court along the right bank downstream from the State Highway 89 bridge begins to flood near this level.
State Route RA at Pointers Creek near the County Road 801 intersection begins flooding near this level.
Brush Creek backs up and private roads begin to flood.

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.
River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
